# UC-13.6.9 · Grafana Image Renderer Timeout and Failure Rate

## Description

PDF and PNG exports for executives depend on headless Chrome; memory pressure and slow datasources manifest as renderer timeouts that block reporting deadlines. Measuring failure rate and p95 duration guides scaling and query tuning.

## Value

Protects automated reporting and compliance packs that rely on rendered Grafana images.

## Implementation

Tag renderer pods uniquely; correlate failures with datasource UID in the same request log line. Increase shared memory limits when Chrome crash signatures appear.

## SPL

```spl
index=observability sourcetype="grafana:rendering" OR (sourcetype="grafana:log" renderer)
| eval fail=if(match(lower(_raw),"(?i)timeout|timed out|500|chrome crashed|navigation failed"),1,0)
| eval ms=coalesce(render_duration_ms, duration_ms)
| bin _time span=15m
| stats count as renders, sum(fail) as failures, perc95(ms) as p95_ms by _time, panel_id, org_id
| eval fail_rate=round(100*failures/nullif(renders,0),2)
| where fail_rate > 5 OR p95_ms > 20000
| sort -fail_rate
```

## Visualization

Line chart (fail_rate), histogram (render ms), top failing dashboard UIDs.

## References

- [Grafana Image Renderer](https://github.com/grafana/grafana-image-renderer)
- [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)
